title: Muhlenbergia minutiflora
taxonomy: Muhlenbergia minutiflora is classified within the family Poaceae. This classification places it among the grasses, as Poaceae is a large and diverse family of monocotyledonous plants. Further, Muhlenbergia minutiflora is a species within the genus Muhlenbergia, which consists of various grass species native to North and South America. The genus Muhlenbergia is itself within the tribe Muhlenbergieae, which is part of the subfamily Muhlenbergioideae. This subfamily is a distinct group of grasses characterized by their inflorescence and seed structure.

intro: Muhlenbergia minutiflora, also known as desert muhly, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Muhlenbergia minutiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to soothe sore throats, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
